summary: Visions of Johanna shines, Iko Iko electrifies, tight set flow
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Visions Of Johanna and an electrifying Iko Iko, backed by Vince’s barrel‑house piano. The second set rides a high‑octane groove with tight Playin > Uncle John’s transitions and a powerful drum/Space jam, ending with a flawless Rain encore.
review:
On March 18, 1995 at the Spectrum, the Dead turned in an energetic performance that still feels fresh for listeners. Jerry’s vocal on Visions Of Johanna was repeatedly called “beautiful” and his lead lines throughout the night earned high praise, especially on the audience recording where fans claim he was “best‑ever”. Vince Welnick’s piano work was a highlight – described as “magnificent” and “barrel‑house”, it lifted It’s All Too Much and gave extra sparkle to the Iko Iko sing‑along. The rhythm section—Bill and Mickey—were tight and even “transfixed,” delivering a 12‑minute drum segment that many reviewers called a favorite moment of any S2 show. While Hell In A Bucket and West L.A. Fadeaway fell flat, the set’s flow stayed natural, with seamless segues like Playin > Uncle John’s keeping the momentum high. The encore closed on a perfect note with Rain, showcasing the band’s ability to turn a solid night into a memorable one.

Overall, the show balances standout moments and occasional missteps, but the positives—especially the vocal‑driven set pieces and Vince’s keyboard brilliance—make it a must‑listen for Deadheads seeking that mid‑’90s Vince era spark.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising Visions Of Johanna, Iko Iko, It’s All Too Much and the drum/space segment; three others are mixed but note no major negative consensus. The balance yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crisp soundboard tape praised as "crispy" and "perfect sounding board" with clear vocals and instruments, capturing the high‑energy performance without drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Soaring vocal on Visions Of Johanna
Tight Playin > Uncle John’s transition and extended drum jam

band_performance.Jerry: Emotional vocal on Visions Of Johanna and stellar lead work throughout; guitar solid but not at his absolute peak, with occasional technical hiccups.
band_performance.Phil: Tight, rhythmic bass foundation; backup vocals on Iko Iko add depth; overall steady presence.
band_performance.Bob: Strong vocal contributions, especially on Iko Iko bridge; adds improvisational groove in Space and rhythmic licks on Ramble On Rose.
band_performance.Brent: Vince's piano described as magnificent and barrel‑house, driving It’s All Too Much and adding sparkle to every jam; also provides notable backing vocals.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight, transfixing drumming that anchors the set; highlighted in a 12‑minute drum segment praised by multiple reviewers.
band_performance.Billy: Provides solid, tight rhythmic backbone alongside Mickey, keeping the groove steady throughout the high‑octane second set.
